_comment: darkmux profile registry — example. Four reference profiles in increasing capacity order: fast → balanced → deep → gpt-oss. A profile's `models[]` are WORKER models (no `role` field — #590); the machine's standing utility/compactor model lives once in the top-level `internal.utility` binding, not per-profile. `default_model` (optional) names a profile's default worker when it lists more than one; with a single model it's implicit. Replace the model ids with what you have downloaded in LMStudio (run `lms ls`).

fast.description: Single-turn tasks. Slim worker — fastest dispatch, lowest RAM.

balanced.description: Mid-range tasks. Predictable behavior across mixed workloads; compaction runs on the machine's internal.utility model.

deep.description: Long agentic tasks. Maximum worker context for fewer compactions.

gpt-oss.description: OpenAI gpt-oss-120b (MXFP4 quantization) for Apple Silicon — worker at 100K. Requires OpenClaw >= v2026.5.9-beta.1 (harmony format / tool-payload fix).
